specification:
Type: infographic

    Purpose: Help students choose the appropriate electronic payment method for different scenarios (Bloom's: Applying, Evaluating)

    Layout: Decision tree with scenario-based guidance

    Top level question: "What kind of payment do you need to make?"

    Branch 1: "Paying a friend or family member"
    - Amount < $1,000 → "Use P2P app (Venmo, Cash App, Zelle)" [Green checkmark]
      - Benefits shown: "Free, instant, convenient"
    - Amount > $1,000 → "Use ACH transfer or check" [Yellow caution]
      - Warning: "P2P apps have transaction limits"

    Branch 2: "Paying a bill"
    - Regular monthly bill → "Set up automatic ACH" [Green checkmark]
      - Benefits: "Free, automatic, never late"
    - One-time bill → "Use bank's bill pay" [Green checkmark]
      - Benefits: "Free, trackable, no stamps"

    Branch 3: "Large purchase (>$10,000)"
    - Time sensitive/real estate → "Wire transfer" [Red caution]
      - Note: "$15-35 fee, irreversible, guaranteed"
    - Not time sensitive → "ACH transfer" [Green checkmark]
      - Note: "Free, takes 1-3 days"

    Branch 4: "International payment"
    - Large amount/urgent → "Wire transfer" [Yellow]
      - Note: "$35-50 fee, same-day"
    - Small amount/not urgent → "PayPal or Wise" [Green]
      - Note: "Lower fees, 1-3 days"

    Branch 5: "Store purchase"
    - In person → "Debit card" [Green checkmark]
      - Note: "Instant, widely accepted"
    - Online → "Credit card preferred" [Blue info]
      - Note: "Better fraud protection"

    Interactive elements:
    - Click any scenario to see a detailed example
    - Hover over payment method to see typical fees and timing
    - Toggle between "lowest cost" and "fastest" recommendations
    - Link to "Learn more" for each payment type

    Visual style:
    - Clean flowchart with rounded boxes
    - Color coding: Green (recommended), Yellow (caution), Red (warning), Blue (info)
    - Icons for each payment method
    - Clear arrows showing decision flow

    Key insights highlighted:
    - "P2P apps are great for friends, not strangers"
    - "Wire transfers are expensive - use sparingly"
    - "ACH is free and sufficient for most needs"
    - "Never wire money to someone you don't know"

    Implementation: Interactive HTML/CSS/JavaScript decision tree
